text: Present situation of gifted education in different countries worldwide is highly diverse. Some countries have very well developed special programmes for gifted students, while in other countries the term of “giftedness” and “gifted children” are not recognised at all. The support of gifted children in most countries is provided by the activities of different organisations. In addition to the regular system of education for the gifted, this article maps the organisations supporting the gifted children. Although the idea of elitism and the notion that the gifted children need no intervention at all is common, even among the educators, this is based on myths and the misconception, which prevent from fully utilising the potential of the gifted. The talents and abilities of the gifted children should be especially noticed and brought to the focus, so that their talents and abilities would not disappear unrecognised. This article is a part of a holistic study on the gifted education and it reports on the diverse methods of educating gifted children in several European countries.
